stemHtml: Air moving over or around large ground obstacles such as mountains can create turbulence in the lee of the terrain feature, which..

options.a: is best counteracted by lowering the altitude so that the aircraft flies close to the ground, as this is where the turbulence will be very weak.
options.b: can be relatively easily recognised as the rotor manifests itself in the form of a cirrus cloud, making it possible to evade the associated turbulence.
options.c: may produce violent and, for smaller aircraft, potentially uncontrollable effects resulting in pitch and / or roll to extreme positions.
options.d: may be strong but as it is very localised it is sufficient if the pilot holds firmly onto the controls for a few seconds to prevent any sudden upset of the aircraft.
